BIOS 6624: Project 3

Ryan Summers

2026-04-01

Load Data

fmhst_raw <- read_csv("/Users/ryan_summers/GitHub/BIOS6624-Class/Project3/DataRaw/frmgham2.csv")
## Rows: 11627 Columns: 39
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## dbl (39): RANDID, SEX, TOTCHOL, AGE, SYSBP, DIABP, CURSMOKE, CIGPDAY, BMI, D...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Data Management & Cleanup

Remove subjects who had event (stroke) at baseline and Filter to 10yrs cutoff. Calculate censored indicator if event doesn’t happen within 10yrs and calculate time to censoring variable.

cutoff_days <- 10*365.25

fmhst_filt <- fmhst_raw %>% 
  # remove subjects who had event prior to baseline assessment
  filter(PERIOD == 1, PREVSTRK == 0) %>%   # baseline cohort
  mutate(
    # time to event or being censored truncated at 10 years
    TIMESTRKorCENS_10y = pmin(TIMESTRK, cutoff_days),

    # event indicator within 10 years
    STROKE_10y = if_else(STROKE == 1 & TIMESTRK <= cutoff_days, 1, 0),
    
    ## all censored subjects
    ## **TIMESTRK includes censored participants if STROKE==0**
    CENSORED_10y = if_else(STROKE_10y == 0, 1, 0),
    
    # convert variables to factors
    SEX = factor(SEX, levels = c(1,2), labels = c("Men", "Women")),
    DIABETES = factor(DIABETES, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    PREVCHD = factor(PREVCHD, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    BPMEDS = factor(BPMEDS, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    CURSMOKE = factor(CURSMOKE, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    STROKE_10y = factor(STROKE_10y, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No-Censored", "Yes")),
    STROKE_bin = ifelse(STROKE_10y == "Yes", 1, 0),
    CENSORED_10y = factor(CENSORED_10y, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    TIMESTRKorCENS_10y_yrs = TIMESTRKorCENS_10y/365.25,
    )

We restricted the primary analysis to baseline risk factors measured at period 1 and modeled incident stroke over the first 10 years of follow-up. This approach aligns predictor measurement more closely with the risk window of interest. We additionally examined how major risk factors changed across the three examination periods to assess whether a time-varying covariate analysis might be informative. If risk factors change materially over time, a baseline-only model may understate or misclassify the relationship between current risk-factor status and stroke hazard. However, time-varying covariate analyses in observational data may also be harder to interpret because updated covariates can be influenced by treatment changes and evolving disease status.

Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

Table1

tbl1 <- fmhst_filt %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = SEX,
    include = c(
      AGE, SYSBP, DIABETES, TOTCHOL, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE,
      BMI, STROKE_10y),
    type = list(
      all_continuous() ~ "continuous2",
      all_categorical() ~ "categorical"),
    statistic = list(
      all_continuous() ~ c(
        "{mean} ({sd})",
        "{median} ({p25}, {p75})",
        "{min} - {max}"),
      all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"),
    digits = list(AGE ~ 0, SYSBP ~ 0),
    missing = "ifany",
    missing_text = "NA",
    missing_stat = "{N_miss} ({p_miss}%)",
    label = list(
      AGE ~ "Age",
      SYSBP ~ "Systolic BP",
      DIABETES ~ "Diabetes",
      TOTCHOL ~ "Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)",
      PREVCHD ~ "Prevalent Coronary Heart Disease",
      BPMEDS ~ "BP Meds",
      CURSMOKE ~ "Smoker",
      STROKE_10y ~ "Event (Stroke)")) %>%
  add_overall() %>% bold_labels() %>% italicize_levels() %>% add_n() %>%
  as_gt() %>%
  gt::fmt_markdown(columns = everything()) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Characteristics of the Framingham Heart Study Cohort") %>% 
  tab_options(
    table.width = pct(70),
    table.font.size = "small",
    heading.align = "left",
    data_row.padding = px(3),
    table.border.top.width = px(2),
    table.border.bottom.width = px(2))

# number of participants
cat("Number of participants in entire cohort", 
   length(unique(fmhst_raw$RANDID)), "\n\n")
## Number of participants in entire cohort 4434
cat("Number of participants in 10yr subset cohort", 
   length(unique(fmhst_filt$RANDID)), "\n\n")
## Number of participants in 10yr subset cohort 4402
hist(fmhst_filt$TOTCHOL)

STROKE indicator is 0 but there’s a time to stroke value????

fmhst_filt %>% 
  filter(STROKE==0 & TIMESTRK <= 10*365.25) %>% 
  select(c(1,24,29,), 36:43)
## # A tibble: 379 × 11
##    RANDID DEATH STROKE TIMESTRK TIMECVD TIMEDTH TIMEHYP TIMESTRKorCENS_10y
##     <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>              <dbl>
##  1  30928     1      0      146     146     146       0                146
##  2  33555     1      0     1442    1442    1442    1442               1442
##  3  54224     1      0      430     430     430     430                430
##  4  63221     1      0      168     168     168       0                168
##  5  76273     1      0     1417    1417    1417    1417               1417
##  6  95541     1      0     2697    2697    2697       0               2697
##  7 100722     1      0     1881    1881    1881    1881               1881

##  9 157585     1      0     1620    1620    1620    1472               1620
## 10 177761     0      0     2911    2911    8766    2911               2911
## # ℹ 369 more rows
## # ℹ 3 more variables: STROKE_10y <fct>, CENSORED_10y <fct>, STROKE_bin <dbl>
# crude incidence
fmhst_filt %>%
  group_by(SEX) %>%
  summarise(
    events = sum(STROKE_10y == "Yes"),
    total = n(),
    risk = round( (events / total)*100,1 ))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##   SEX   events total  risk
##   <fct>  <int> <int> <dbl>
## 1 Men       49  1930   2.5
## 2 Women     62  2472   2.5

Kaplan-Meier Curve

km.fit <- survfit(Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_10y == "Yes") ~ BPMEDS, 
                  data = fmhst_filt)

ggsurvplot(km.fit,
           conf.int = F,
           risk.table = T,
           ylim = c(0.97,1),
           xlab = "Time (Days)",
           ylab = "Stroke-free probability",
           tables.height = 0.2,
           tables.theme = theme_cleantable(),
            # Color palettes. Use custom color: c("#E7B800", "#2E9FDF"),
           # or brewer color (e.g.: "Dark2"), or ggsci color (e.g.: "jco")
           palette = c("#E7B800", "#2E9FDF"),
           ggtheme = theme_bw() )
## Warning: Removed 30 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_step()`).
## Warning: Removed 19 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).
## Warning: Removed 30 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_step()`).
## Warning: Removed 19 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • colour : "Strata"

# create grouped versions of variables
fmhst_plot <- fmhst_filt %>%
  mutate(
    SBP_cat = if_else(SYSBP >= 140, "BP≥140", "BP<140"),
    AGE_cat = if_else(AGE >= 60, "Age≥60", "Age<60"),
    TOTCHOL_cat = if_else(TOTCHOL >= 240, "Chol≥240", "Chol<240"),
    BMI_cat = if_else(BMI >= 30, "BMI≥30", "BMI<30")
    ) %>%
  select(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_10y, SBP_cat, DIABETES, PREVCHD, AGE_cat) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_10y),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "group") %>% 
    mutate(
    variable = factor(variable,
      levels = c("AGE_cat", "SBP_cat", "DIABETES", "PREVCHD"),
      labels = c("Age", "Systolic BP", "Diabetes", "Prior CHD")
    ))

# fit survival object
fit <- survfit(Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_10y == "Yes") ~ group + variable, 
  data = fmhst_plot)

# plot
p <- ggsurvplot_facet(
  fit,
  data = fmhst_plot,
  facet.by = "variable",
  conf.int = FALSE,
  ylim = c(0.97,1),
  xlab = "Time (Days)",
  ylab = "Stroke-free probability",
  ggtheme = theme_bw())

p + theme(legend.position = "bottom")
## Warning: Removed 249 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_step()`).
## Warning: Removed 176 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

# create grouped versions of variables
fmhst_plot2 <- fmhst_filt %>%
  mutate(
    SBP_cat = if_else(SYSBP >= 140, "BP≥140", "BP<140"),
    AGE_cat = if_else(AGE >= 60, "Age≥60", "Age<60"),
    TOTCHOL_cat = if_else(TOTCHOL >= 240, "Chol≥240", "Chol<240"),
    BMI_cat = if_else(BMI >= 30, "BMI≥30", "BMI<30")
    ) %>%
  select(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_10y, TOTCHOL_cat, BMI_cat, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_10y),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "group") %>% 
    mutate(
    variable = factor(variable,
      levels = c("TOTCHOL_cat", "BMI_cat", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE"),
      labels = c("Total Chol", "BMI", "BP Meds", "Smoker")
    ))

# fit survival object
fit2 <- survfit(Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_10y == "Yes") ~ group + variable, 
  data = fmhst_plot2)

# plot
p2 <- ggsurvplot_facet(
  fit2,
  data = fmhst_plot2,
  facet.by = "variable",
  conf.int = FALSE,
  ylim = c(0.97,1),
  xlab = "Time (Days)",
  ylab = "Stroke-free probability",
  ggtheme = theme_bw())

p2 + theme(legend.position = "bottom")
## Warning: Removed 46 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_step()`).
## Warning: Removed 28 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

Distributions of Time to Event by Sex

# time-to-event variables and labels
# time_vars <- c(
#   TIMEHYP  = "Days Baseline-Inc Hypertension",
#   TIMEAP   = "Days Baseline-Inc Angina",
#   TIMEMI   = "Days Baseline-Inc Hosp MI",
#   TIMEMIFC = "Days Baseline-Inc MI-Fatal CHD",
#   TIMESTRK = "Days Baseline-Inc Stroke",
#   TIMECHD  = "Days Baseline-Inc Any CHD",
#   TIMECVD  = "Days Baseline-Inc CVD",
#   TIMEDTH  = "Days Baseline-Death")

time_vars <- c(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y = "Follow-up time (days)")

# build summary table
time_tbl <- fmhst_filt %>%
  group_by(SEX) %>%
  summarise(
    `Time to Event` = "Follow-up time (days)",
    N = sum(!is.na(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y)),
    NMiss = sum(is.na(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y)),
    Mean = mean(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, na.rm = TRUE),
    Std = sd(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min = min(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, na.rm = TRUE),
    P25 = quantile(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median = median(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, na.rm = TRUE),
    P75 = quantile(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") %>%
  # indent labels within sex groups
  mutate(SEX = if_else(duplicated(SEX), "", as.character(SEX))) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "SEX") %>%
  tab_header(title = "Distributions of Time to Event by Sex") %>%
  cols_label(
    `Time to Event` = "Time to Event",
    N = "N",
    NMiss = "NMiss",
    Mean = "Mean",
    Std = "Std",
    Min = "Min",
    P25 = "P25",
    Median = "Median",
    P75 = "P75",
    Max = "Max") %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(Mean, Std, Min, P25, Median, P75, Max),
    decimals = 0) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "left",
    columns = c(`Time to Event`)) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(N, NMiss, Mean, Std, Min, P25, Median, P75, Max)) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(16),
    heading.align = "left",
    data_row.padding = px(6),
    row_group.font.weight = "normal",
    column_labels.font.weight = "normal",
    table.border.top.width = px(1),
    table.border.bottom.width = px(1))

time_tbl

Time to Event N NMiss Mean Std Min P25 Median P75 Max

Primary Analysis

cox.male.model <- coxph(
  Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +  
  TOTCHOL + BMI + CURSMOKE + BPMEDS + PREVCHD, 
                   data = subset(fmhst_filt, SEX == "Men"))
summary(cox.male.model)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     SYSBP + DIABETES + TOTCHOL + BMI + CURSMOKE + BPMEDS + PREVCHD, 
##     data = subset(fmhst_filt, SEX == "Men"))
## 
##   n= 1897, number of events= 48 
##    (33 observations deleted due to missingness)
## 
##                  coef exp(coef)  se(coef)      z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE          0.065578  1.067776  0.019741  3.322 0.000894 ***
## SYSBP        0.031818  1.032330  0.006112  5.206 1.93e-07 ***
## DIABETESYes  1.634803  5.128446  0.428783  3.813 0.000137 ***
## TOTCHOL      0.001655  1.001656  0.003196  0.518 0.604589    
## BMI         -0.006383  0.993638  0.044239 -0.144 0.885279    
## CURSMOKEYes  0.686304  1.986361  0.324363  2.116 0.034357 *  
## BPMEDSYes    0.712323  2.038721  0.562826  1.266 0.205650    
## PREVCHDYes   0.050886  1.052203  0.538883  0.094 0.924769    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##             exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE            1.0678     0.9365    1.0273     1.110
## SYSBP          1.0323     0.9687    1.0200     1.045
## DIABETESYes    5.1284     0.1950    2.2131    11.884
## TOTCHOL        1.0017     0.9983    0.9954     1.008
## BMI            0.9936     1.0064    0.9111     1.084
## CURSMOKEYes    1.9864     0.5034    1.0519     3.751
## BPMEDSYes      2.0387     0.4905    0.6765     6.144
## PREVCHDYes     1.0522     0.9504    0.3659     3.026
## 
## Concordance= 0.806  (se = 0.03 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 71.04  on 8 df,   p=3e-12
## Wald test            = 79.86  on 8 df,   p=5e-14
## Score (logrank) test = 102  on 8 df,   p=<2e-16

Proportional assumption - low p-values indicates variable has time dependency “the proportionality assumption does not check linearity - the Cox PH model is semi-parametric and thus makes no assumption as to the form of the hazard. The proportionality assumption is that the hazard rate of an individual is relatively constant in time” * semi-parametric = don’t have to choose probability dist’n for survival time

cox.zph(cox.male.model)
##           chisq df     p
## AGE      0.5054  1 0.477
## SYSBP    1.8076  1 0.179
## DIABETES 2.8116  1 0.094
## TOTCHOL  0.1186  1 0.731
## BMI      1.2801  1 0.258
## CURSMOKE 0.0836  1 0.772
## BPMEDS   0.1167  1 0.733
## PREVCHD  0.4123  1 0.521
## GLOBAL   6.0505  8 0.642
plot(cox.zph(cox.male.model))

# # different arbitrary risk profiles
# risk_profiles <- data.frame(
#   profile_name = c("Low Risk", "Moderate Risk", "High Risk"),
#   AGE = c(55, 65, 75),
#   DIABETES = c(0, 0, 1),
#   SYSBP = c(120, 140, 160),
#   CURSMOKE = c(0, 1, 1),
#   BMI = c(25, 28, 32))

# baseline survival at 10 years 
baseline_surv <- survfit(cox.male.model)
summary(baseline_surv, times = 10*365.25)
## Call: survfit(formula = cox.male.model)
## 
##  time n.risk n.event survival std.err lower 95% CI upper 95% CI
##  3652   1643      48     0.99 0.00318        0.984        0.996
# survival to probability of stroke
#risk_profiles$prob_stroke_10yr <- 1 - summary_baseline$surv
